rulings.Case Title: FLICK, INC. VS. MING CHAO LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING OF CLAIM OF EXEMPTION AS TO RESPONDENT MARIA FERNANDA DEL GAISO, ONLY
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, line 9 HEARING OF CLAIM OF EXEMPTION AS TO RESPONDENT MARIA FERNANDA DEL GAISO, ONLY This matter is on calendar to address the claim of exemption filed by Maria Fernanda Del Gaiso. Hearing required. Ms. Del Gaiso has not provided a financial statement form showing that the funds are necessary for her support. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. If the court adopts the tentative ruling, it will execute a judicial council form of order reflecting the ruling. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: RAMY KAUFLER EDEN VS. GAWFCO ENTERPRISES, INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF RAMY EDEN'S Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Consent Judgment The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)." The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MARIA MONICA SAGULLO VS. EUGENE A. CHANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, line 4, DEFENDANT EUGENE CHANG'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT On December 18, 2024, Defendant Eugene A. Chang's demurrer to complaint was overruled in its entirety. Defendant brings a renewed demurrer on grounds that the court's December ruling (that Plaintiff sufficiently alleged breach of contract because the complaint stated the loan contracts were oral and memorialized in emails) constitutes a new circumstance under CCP 1008(b). (MPA 3:13-15.) Defendant now renews his demurrer on the grounds that the first cause of action for breach of contract does not contain the terms of the written contract set out verbatim in the body of the complaint, nor contain a copy of a written contract attached and incorporated by reference. (Id. at 4:8-10.) The court overrules Chang's demurrer. The court's ruling is not a new fact and the complaint to which he has already demurred once is the operative pleading. His reliance on Gravillises v. Coldwell Banker (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 761,772, is inapposite on these facts. Gravillises was analyzed under CCP 1008(a) and the court held the new circumstance under that section was that a judge made an abrupt reversal of his original decision without a notice or hearing. That circumstance is not present here. Here, Defendant seeks a renewed demurrer on facts that were already in existence when he filed his original demurrer. There is no basis to grant reconsideration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. VS. WOLLACH FAMILY DENTAL, PC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, line 5, PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'S Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order Plaintiff's unopposed motion against defendant The Omene Dental Group, LLC, dba Phoenixville Dental ("Omene Dental") for an order assigning accounts, accounts receivable, rights to payment of money, contingent rights, contract rights, deposits and deposit accounts, claims against their parties, and monies due from third parties, is granted. Omene Dental is restrained from the sale, alienation, mortgage, lien, encumbrance, advancement, cashing or negotiation, or receipt or exploitation of the above-described accounts. Omene Dental is ordered to turn over documentary evidence of any of the accounts to the Sheriff of the City and County of San Francisco as identified in the moving papers.
